summary: Crystal‑clear soundboard capture of an ’85 Fox Theatre show with smoking Stranger, epic Sailor > Saint > Crazy Fingers jam and a standout Franklin’s Tower/Other One second set
review: Fans rave that this is one of the best‑sounding SBDs from the mid‑80s – crystal clear, no hiss and a pristine mix that lets every instrument shine. Musically the concert is a high point: a hot Stranger opener, a superb Sailor > Saint > Crazy Fingers sequence, smoking Half Step, multiple Franklin’s Tower renditions and a soaring Other One jam make the second set unforgettable.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews, most 5‑star, all praising both performance and sound quality; only minor lyrical slip noted. Weighted show (70 %) and recording (30 %) scores are both excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crystal‑clear, superior mix with no hiss or distortion; many call it the best soundboard of this era and note flawless mastering throughout both sets.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is short but packed with fire (Stranger, Friend of the Devil); the second set launches into an extended Sailor‑Saint‑Crazy Fingers jam, flows through Half Step and Franklin’s Tower, and climaxes with a soaring Other One. The pacing is praised as tight and exhilarating.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone described as perfect; vocals on Friend of the Devil highlighted as strong, though one reviewer notes a lyrical stumble during Crazy Fingers.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass called “rock solid” by listeners, providing a firm foundation throughout the jams.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby’s scream after the bridge in Hell in a Bucket is celebrated as a classic Weir move.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) praised as rock‑solid; overall keyboards noted as “nice” and integral to the jam flow.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill's drumming described as solid and “drippy” during the Crazy Fingers segment, keeping the groove tight.
